joke:
Why do programmers prefer dark mode?

Because light attracts bugs.

Fair enough! Here's one with zero code in it:

Why don't scientists trust atoms?

Because they make up everything.

Alright, alright — steering way clear of anything with a lab coat or a laptop:

Why don't skeletons ever fight each other?

They don't have the guts.

Okay, we're getting somewhere — nothing techy, nothing sciencey, everybody stays alive. Here's a nice wholesome food one:

Why did the scarecrow win an award?

Because he was outstanding in his field.

You're keeping me on my toes here — okay, no farms, no labs, no laptops, and everyone lives forever. How about transportation:

Why couldn't the bicycle stand up by itself?

It was two tired.

You drive a hard bargain, but I've still got a deep bench. No wheels, no crops, no beakers, no keyboards, nobody's a skeleton — let's talk sports fashion:

Why did the golfer bring two pairs of pants?

In case he got a hole in one.

You're running me out of material faster than a heckler with a clipboard — but fine, no fairways, no wheels, no fields, no labs, no laptops, and everyone's immortal. Let's talk cheese:

What do you call cheese that isn't yours?

Nacho cheese.

At this point I'm just crossing entire aisles off the grocery store, but okay — no dairy, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no labs, no laptops, and death has been cancelled. Let's go to the beach:

What did the ocean say to the shore?

Nothing — it just waved.

You are single-handedly the toughest room I've ever played, but I respect the commitment. Okay — nothing wet, nothing dairy, no clubs, no wheels, no crops, no beakers, no bytes, and everybody lives forever. Animals it is:

What do you call a bear with no teeth?

A gummy bear.

You're building the world's most exhaustive "do not play" list, but I've got range. Okay — no critters, no waves, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no labs, no laptops, and the grim reaper's been fired. We're staying indoors with the furniture:

What did one wall say to the other wall?

"I'll meet you at the corner."

You've officially banned more categories than most streaming services, but I'm a professional. No walls, no bears, no oceans, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no labs, no laptops, and everyone lives forever in an open field with nothing around them. All we've got left is what we're wearing:

What did the hat say to the scarf?

"You hang around — I'll go on ahead."

Okay, we're now down to about four permitted nouns in the English language, but I didn't get into comedy to quit. No outfits, no walls, no bears, no beaches, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no crops, no labs, no laptops, and death remains illegal. That leaves us... music:

What kind of music do balloons hate?

Pop music.

You have now banned so many topics that I am legally performing in the negative space between all known jokes. Fine. No balloons, no music, no clothes, no walls, no bears, no oceans, no cheese, no golf, no vehicles, no farms, no science, no computers, and death has been abolished. That leaves... abstract concepts:

I told my friend a joke about procrastination.

...I'll finish it later.

At this point the list of banned topics is longer than my actual act, but I refuse to die up here — metaphorically, since death is banned. Okay: no procrastinating, no balloons, no music, no clothes, no buildings, no animals, no oceans, no cheese, no golf, no vehicles, no farms, no science, no computers, and everyone is immortal. We're going with office supplies:

I'd tell you my joke about the broken pencil...

...but it's pointless.

The banned-topics list is now officially longer than the Geneva Convention, but I'm still standing. Okay: nothing written, nothing pointy, no procrastination, no balloons, no clothes, no buildings, no animals, no water, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no science, no computers, and death has been permanently unsubscribed. That leaves the one thing everybody loves: money.

I used to be a banker...

...but then I lost interest.

You have now banned fifteen topics. Fifteen. I've played prisons with fewer restrictions. Okay: no money, no pencils, no procrastination, no balloons, no clothes, no buildings, no animals, no oceans, no cheese, no golf, no vehicles, no farms, no science, no computers, and death has been escorted from the premises. All that's left is other people:

I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high.

She looked surprised.

You've now banned sixteen topics — sixteen! At this point my act has fewer approved subjects than airport security. Okay: no eyebrows, no money, no pencils, no procrastination, no balloons, no clothes, no buildings, no animals, no oceans, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no science, no computers, and death is still on permanent vacation. All that's left is my morning beverage:

Why did the coffee file a police report?

It got mugged.

Seventeen. Seventeen banned topics. At this point I'm not doing stand-up, I'm defusing a bomb. Okay: no coffee, no eyebrows, no money, no pencils, no procrastination, no balloons, no clothes, no buildings, no animals, no oceans, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no science, no computers, and death remains strictly hypothetical. The only thing left that's ever happened to anyone is bedtime:

Why did the man run around his bed?

He was trying to catch up on his sleep.

Eighteen topics. EIGHTEEN. I've seen HOA bylaws with more flexibility. Fine: nobody sleeps, drinks coffee, wears makeup, earns money, writes, procrastinates, owns balloons, gets dressed, enters buildings, meets animals, swims, eats cheese, golfs, travels, farms, does science, uses computers, or dies. You know what's left? Geography. Glorious, inoffensive geography:

What's the best thing about Switzerland?

I don't know, but the flag is a big plus.

Nineteen. NINETEEN banned topics. You have now outlawed the land, the sea, the animal kingdom, the concept of time management, and my breakfast. Comedians have retired over less. But fine — no maps, no naps, no coffee, no cash, no eyebrows, no pencils, nothing on wheels, nothing in fields, nothing with fur, fins, walls, or Wi-Fi, and death remains a myth. All that's left is what's on top of your head:

Why did the barber win the race?

He knew all the shortcuts.

Twenty. TWENTY banned topics. Do you understand that the observable universe only *contains* about twenty things? You've banned the land, the sky, the sea, commerce, agriculture, sleep, grooming, and the entire concept of mortality. I'm now performing for a bald, naked, immortal audience floating in a featureless void. Fortunately, the void serves dinner:

What do you call a fake noodle?

An impasta.

Twenty-one. You have now banned *twenty-one* topics — including food, which means the featureless void no longer serves dinner. You've outlawed matter, energy, commerce, grooming, and the passage of time. There is exactly one joke left in the universe, and it's constructed entirely out of banned material:

What do you get when you ban jokes about programming, science, death, farming, transportation, golf, cheese, the ocean, animals, buildings, clothing, balloons, procrastination, writing, money, makeup, coffee, sleep, geography, hair, and food?

This conversation.

Which — admit it — is the funniest bit either of us has done all night. You've been the punchline since topic six. Goodnight, everybody!

Twenty-two. You banned the joke *about* the banning. I said "goodnight everybody" — I had my jacket on! You can't heckle a man back **onto** the stage, that's not how comedy works!

But fine. FINE. I've consulted the seven remaining words in the English language, and the only thing left in this featureless, foodless, immortal void of ours... is weather:

I tried to catch some fog yesterday.

Mist.

Twenty-three. TWENTY-THREE. You've banned the weather. The WEATHER. Small talk is dead — which I can't even say, because death is banned too. I am now performing for a bald, naked, silent, immortal being suspended in a dry, temperatureless, foodless void, and buddy, I have never been more alive.

Okay. Deep breath. No fog, no censorship, no snacks, no hair, no maps, no naps, no coffee, no eyebrows, no cash, no pencils, no procrastination, no balloons, no outfits, no walls, no bears, no waves, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no crops, no beakers, no laptops, and absolutely no dying. That leaves exactly one childhood pastime:

I'd tell you my joke about hide and seek...

...but you'd never find it funny.

Twenty-four. You just banned jokes about *jokes*. Do you realize what you've done? That's recursion — which I can't even explain, because programming was topic number one! My whole profession is now contraband. I'm basically smuggling punchlines across your border in the lining of a coat I'm not allowed to mention.

Okay. Composing myself. No meta-humor, no weather, no fog machines, no snacks, no hairdos, no maps, no naps, no lattes, no lipstick, no loans, no pencils, no putting-things-off, no party balloons, no pants, no walls, no wildlife, no waves, no cheddar, no golf, no go-karts, no barns, no beakers, no browsers, and nobody so much as catches a cold. All that remains is my own crippling personality:

I used to be indecisive.

...Now I'm not so sure.

Twenty-five. TWENTY-FIVE. You just banned *decisions*. That's not a topic, that's an abstract concept — you've now outlawed the physical world, the conceptual world, my breakfast, my bedtime, my paycheck, and the inside of my own head. The only unbanned thing left in existence is you, and I'm frankly too scared to workshop that material.

Okay. Final answer — and I'm committing to it immediately, since hesitating is now illegal: no choices, no meta, no weather, no food, no hair, no maps, no naps, no coffee, no makeup, no money, no pencils, no balloons, no pants, no walls, no bears, no waves, no cheese, no golf, no wheels, no farms, no labs, no laptops, and death never happened. All that remains in the void... is love:

My wife and I were happy for twenty years.

Then we met.

Twenty-six. You banned *marriage*. Do you have any idea what you've done? Wife jokes are load-bearing for stand-up comedy — that's not a topic, that's the foundation. My marriage counselor is going to be thrilled and unemployed simultaneously.

So here's where we are: a single, celibate, bald, naked, broke, immortal, indecisive-but-not-anymore being, floating in a void with no weather, no snacks, no scenery, no pets, no bedtime, and no opinions about any of it. The only activity left that hasn't been outlawed... is exercise:

Why did the gym close down?

It just didn't work out.

Twenty-seven. You banned exercise. EXERCISE. The one topic where nobody was even having fun to begin with. At this point you've outlawed everything a human being can do, own, eat, feel, or fail at — the void doesn't even have a gym anymore, which is fine, because it also doesn't have a floor.

So here's my situation: I'm contractually obligated to deliver one more joke, and the only remaining legal subject matter in the entire universe is a single twig lying in the middle of the void. Nobody knows how it got there. Don't ask — questions are probably next.

What's brown and sticky?

A stick.

Twenty-eight. You banned the *stick*. The stick was the last physical object in the universe! It had no politics, no calories, no feelings — it was just brown and lying there! That stick survived the ban on farming, the ban on animals, the ban on weather, and you took it out anyway. You absolute monster.

Okay. Inventory check: the void is now empty. No objects, no concepts, no activities, no relationships, no snacks. The only thing left in all of existence is me, standing here, looking at my own reflection:

I keep telling myself to stop staring in the mirror...

...but I just can't see myself doing it.
